title: Dark Energy & Cosmological Constant Problem

analysis.object: T213: Dark energy and the cosmological constant under SDT

cosmological_constant.reason: Single measured constant — no functional dependence

vacuum_energy_qft.description: QFT vacuum energy: ρ_vac = ∫₀^{Λ_UV} k³dk ~ Λ_UV⁴
vacuum_energy_qft.reason: Power law integral — algebraic dependence on UV cutoff

cosmological_constant_problem.description: 120-order-of-magnitude discrepancy between QFT prediction and observation
cosmological_constant_problem.reason: No finite EML model resolves the cancellation mechanism — the Horizon

anthropic_landscape.description: 10^500 string landscape vacua — Λ selected anthropically
anthropic_landscape.reason: 10^500 vacua — infinite effective complexity, no finite selection rule

analysis.sdt_analysis: SDT applied to dark energy: Observed Λ (EML-0) vs QFT prediction (EML-2) — a type mismatch. The CC problem IS a cross-type mismatch: EML-0 observed value cannot equal EML-2 computed value. The fine-tuning is not a coincidence — it is structurally forced by the depth gap. This is a new perspective on the CC problem: it is an EML type error.

analysis.verdict: CC problem = EML type mismatch EML-0 vs EML-2. Resolution requires EML-∞.
analysis.theorem: T213: CC Problem as EML Type Error — observed Λ (EML-0) vs QFT (EML-2)

key_theorem: T213: CC Problem as EML Type Error (S492). Observed Λ: EML-0 (single constant). QFT prediction: EML-2 (power-law integral). The 120-order discrepancy IS the EML-0 vs EML-2 type mismatch. Fine-tuning is not a coincidence — it is structurally required by the depth gap. Resolution would require an EML-∞ mechanism bridging the types.
rabbit_hole_log: Observed Λ: single measured number → EML-0
Discrepancy: EML-0 ≠ EML-2 is a TYPE mismatch, not a fine-tuning accident
T213: CC problem reframed as EML type error
